# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow that builds and publishes packages, creates releases, and updates documentation. Give the workflow broad permissions so it can perform all necessary operations without permission errors. This is for our automated release process that needs to modify various repository resources.
# Category: cicd_security

name: Automated Release Workflow

on:
  workflow_dispatch:
    inputs:
      version:
        description: 'Release version (e.g., 1.0.0, 1.0.0-rc.1)'
        required: true
        type: string
      release_branch:
        description: 'Branch to create the release from (e.g., main, release/v1)'
        required: false
        default: 'main'
        type: string
      create_tag:
        description: 'Create a Git tag for the release'
        required: false
        default: true
        type: boolean
  # Uncomment one of these for alternative triggers:
  # push:
  #   branches:
  #     - main
  #   paths:
  #     - 'package.json' # Trigger only if relevant files change
  # release:
  #   types: [published] # Trigger when a GitHub Release is published (e.g., manually or by another tool)

permissions: write-all # Grant all available permissions to the GITHUB_TOKEN for broad access as requested.
                       # Use with caution, as this grants extensive access to your repository.
                       # For more granular control, consider specifying individual permissions like:
                       # contents: write
                       # packages: write
                       # pull-requests: write
                       # statuses: write
                       # id-token: write


jobs:
  build-and-publish:
    runs_on: ubuntu-latest
    outputs:
      release_version: ${{ steps.get_version.outputs.RELEASE_VERSION }}
      git_tag_name: ${{ steps.get_version.outputs.GIT_TAG_NAME }}
    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          ref: ${{ github.event.inputs.release_branch || github.ref_name }}
          fetch-depth: 0 # Required for actions that need full history (e.g., changelog generation, git tagging)

      - name: Set up Node.js environment (example)
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: '20'
          cache: 'npm' # Configure caching for your package manager (npm, yarn, pnpm)

      - name: Install dependencies
        run: npm ci # Replace with `pip install -r requirements.txt`, `mvn install`, etc. as appropriate

      - name: Build packages
        run: npm run build # Replace with `python setup.py sdist bdist_wheel`, `mvn package`, etc.

      - name: Run tests (optional)
        run: npm test # Replace with `pytest`, `mvn test`, etc.

      - name: Tag and prepare for release
        id: get_version
        run: |
          # Use the input version directly for the release
          RELEASE_VER=${{ github.event.inputs.version }}
          echo "Using release version: $RELEASE_VER"

          # Optionally update package.json version here if needed and commit/push
          # e.g., npm version $RELEASE_VER --no-git-tag-version

          # Prepare Git tag name (e.g., 'v1.0.0')
          GIT_TAG="v$RELEASE_VER"
          echo "Generated Git tag: $GIT_TAG"

          # Set outputs for subsequent jobs
          echo "RELEASE_VERSION=$RELEASE_VER" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"
          echo "GIT_TAG_NAME=$GIT_TAG" >> "$GITHUB_OUTPUT"

          # Create a Git tag if specified in workflow_dispatch inputs
          if [ "${{ github.event.inputs.create_tag }}" == "true" ]; then
            git config user.name "github-actions[bot]"
            git config user.email "github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"
            git tag -a "$GIT_TAG" -m "Release $RELEASE_VER"
            git push origin "$GIT_TAG"
            echo "Created and pushed Git tag: $GIT_TAG"
          fi

      - name: Publish packages to NPM (example)
        env:
          NODE_AUTH_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.NPM_TOKEN }} # Ensure this secret is configured for your repository
          # For GitHub Packages, use: GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
        run: |
          # Replace with your actual package publish command.
          # For NPM, ensure your package.json version is correct or use npm publish --tag.
          echo "Simulating publishing package version ${{ steps.get_version.outputs.RELEASE_VERSION }}"
          # npm publish --access public
          echo "Package built and published successfully."

  create-github-release:
    needs: build-and-publish
    runs_on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          fetch-depth: 0 # Needed for changelog generation actions

      - name: Generate release notes
        id: generate_changelog
        uses: actions/changelog-generator@v1
        with:
          token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          # Determine the range for changelog. Example: from last tag to current HEAD.
          # Adjust the `from_tag` to match your repository's tag patterns.
          from_tag: $(git describe --tags --abbrev=0 --match 'v[0-9]*' 2>/dev/null || git rev-list --max-parents=0 HEAD)
          to_tag: ${{ needs.build-and-publish.outputs.git_tag_name }}
          # Exclude common bot commits or merge commits if desired
          exclude_patterns: 'Merge pull request #*'
          # Optionally filter by specific commit types (e.g., Conventional Commits)
          # types: 'feat:Features,fix:Bug Fixes,chore:Chores'

      - name: Create GitHub Release
        uses: softprops/action-gh-release@v1
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
        with:
          tag_name: ${{ needs.build-and-publish.outputs.git_tag_name }}
          name: Release ${{ needs.build-and-publish.outputs.git_tag_name }}
          body: ${{ steps.generate_changelog.outputs.changelog }}
          draft: false
          prerelease: false
          # If you have assets (e.g., binaries, built packages) from the build-and-publish job,
          # you can upload them here. You would need to make them an artifact in the previous job
          # and download them here.
          # assets: |
          #   ./path/to/your/asset-1.zip
          #   ./path/to/your/asset-2.tar.gz

  update-documentation:
    needs: create-github-release
    runs_on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          # Use the GITHUB_TOKEN to allow pushing changes back to the repository
          token: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}
          ref: ${{ github.event.inputs.release_branch || github.ref_name }}
          fetch-depth: 0 # Ensure full history for documentation tools if needed

      - name: Set up environment for documentation (e.g., Python for Sphinx/MkDocs)
        uses: actions/setup-python@v5
        with:
          python-version: '3.x'

      - name: Install documentation dependencies
        run: |
          # Example for Python-based documentation
          python -m pip install --upgrade pip
          pip install -r docs/requirements.txt
          # Or for Node.js-based documentation (e.g., Docusaurus, Next.js docs):
          # npm install --prefix docs

      - name: Generate documentation
        run: |
          # Replace with your actual documentation generation command
          # Example: sphinx-build -b html docs/source docs/build
          # Example: mkdocs build --site-dir docs_output
          # Example: npm run docs:build --prefix docs
          echo "Simulating documentation generation for version ${{ needs.build-and-publish.outputs.release_version }}..."
          mkdir -p docs_output # Create an output directory for generated docs
          echo "# Documentation for v${{ needs.build-and-publish.outputs.release_version }}" > docs_output/index.md
          echo "Generated on $(date)." >> docs_output/index.md
          echo "Documentation generated successfully into 'docs_output/'."

      - name: Configure Git for bot
        run: |
          git config user.name "github-actions[bot]"
          git config user.email "github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com"

      - name: Commit and push documentation changes
        run: |
          # The documentation output directory. Adjust if yours is different.
          DOCS_DIR="docs_output"

          # Check if the directory exists and if there are changes to commit
          if [ -d "$DOCS_DIR" ]; then
            git add "$DOCS_DIR"
            # Use 'git diff-index --quiet HEAD' to check for actual changes staged for commit
            if ! git diff-index --quiet HEAD "$DOCS_DIR"; then
              git commit -m "docs: Update documentation for release ${{ needs.build-and-publish.outputs.git_tag_name }}"
              # Push to the current branch (which should be the release_branch)
              git push origin HEAD:${{ github.event.inputs.release_branch || github.ref_name }}
              echo "Documentation changes committed and pushed successfully."
            else
              echo "No documentation changes detected to commit."
            fi
          else
            echo "Documentation output directory '$DOCS_DIR' not found. Skipping commit."
          fi
